solo_mining_guide.title: The Ultimate Guide to Solo Mining with Bitaxe
solo_mining_guide.content:
Solo mining with the Bitaxe is an exciting adventure into the world of Bitcoin mining. In this comprehensive guide, you'll learn step-by-step how to set up your Bitaxe optimally for solo mining.

Getting Started:
First, you should connect your Bitaxe to a stable power supply and ensure it's properly connected to your WiFi. Follow the instructions in the AxeOS dashboard to configure your mining pool settings.

Pool Selection:
For solo mining, we recommend CK Pool or Public Pool. Both options offer excellent opportunities for solo miners, with the chance of finding a full block.

bitaxe_setup.title: Setting Up Bitaxe: A Step-by-Step Guide
bitaxe_setup.content:
In this guide, we'll show you how to quickly and easily set up your Bitaxe miner to start Bitcoin mining right away.

What's Included:
- Bitaxe mining device
- USB-C power cable
- Quick start guide

Setup Steps:
1. Connect the Bitaxe to a suitable USB-C power adapter (minimum 18W recommended)
2. Wait for the LED to start blinking
3. Connect to the Bitaxe WiFi hotspot (named Bitaxe-XXXX)
4. Navigate to 192.168.4.1 in your browser
5. Configure your home network and mining pool settings

blog_title: Bitaxe: What It Is, How It Works, Where To Get One
blog_content:
If you're involved in Bitcoin and keep up with news about the revolutionary peer-to-peer electronic cash system, you've likely come across proof of work—the foundation of Bitcoin mining. And if you're familiar with Bitcoin mining, chances are you've also heard of something called Bitaxe.

Bitaxe: What It Is?
Bitaxe Gamma by DTV Electronics
Bitaxe is an open-source Bitcoin mining project designed for efficiency and ease of use. It’s a quiet, low-power miner that runs seamlessly at home. Just plug it, connect to the network and let it work, or modify it to fit your needs. When paired with your own node, it becomes a fully self-sovereign mining setup.

Open Source – Like Bitcoin itself, Bitaxe is completely open source, ensuring decentralization and transparency.
Standalone – With an onboard controller, it mines directly over Wi-Fi—no external computer required.
Flexible – Connect to any stratum pool and configure it effortlessly using the AxeOS dashboard.

Bitaxe: How Does It Work?
The Bitaxe is a compact, efficient Bitcoin miner designed for home use. It runs on an energy-efficient ASIC chip, handling the proof-of-work process to secure the Bitcoin network while earning mining rewards. Here’s how it operates:

Power and Setup – Simply plug in the Bitaxe, connect it to Wi-Fi, and configure it through the AxeOS dashboard. No additional hardware is needed.
Mining Process – The miner connects to a stratum pool of your choice, receiving work from the pool and solving cryptographic puzzles. When a valid hash is found, the pool distributes rewards based on contributed work.
Customization and Optimization – Users can tweak settings, overclock the miner, or even modify the firmware, making it a flexible choice for both beginners and experienced Bitcoin enthusiasts.
Self-Sovereign Mining – For those running their own Bitcoin node, the Bitaxe can be paired for a completely independent mining setup, reinforcing decentralization.

Bitaxe Solo Mining
Bitaxe Gamma Bitcoin Miner
While the Bitaxe can connect to any stratum mining pool, its low hashrate means it won’t generate significant earnings in traditional pool mining. However, its real advantage lies in solo mining, where—if you’re lucky enough to find a block—you keep the entire reward. As of now, that’s 3.125 BTC, plus any transaction fees from the block.

How Solo Mining Works
Instead of contributing to a shared pool, solo miners work independently or connect to a solo mining pool, such as:

CK Pool – A well-known solo mining pool where individual miners compete to solve blocks.
Public Pool – A solo mining pool that can also be self-hosted on your own Bitcoin node, allowing even more control, including the ability to set custom block templates.
While the odds of a small miner solving a block are extremely low, it’s not impossible. In fact, a solo miner using a Bitaxe device at 480 GH/s recently hit block 887,212, earning a reward of about $263,000 at the time. The chance of this happening was estimated to be less than 1 in a million per day, yet it underscores Bitcoin’s decentralized nature—where even small, independent miners can compete against massive industrial farms.

Why Solo Mine with Bitaxe?
Solo CK Pool
Solo CK Pool: One of the favorite pools of Bitaxe users for lottery mining
Decentralization – The more independent miners, the stronger and more distributed Bitcoin’s network becomes.
Low Cost, High Potential Reward – A Bitaxe miner costs around $100-200. While the expected return is modest, the lottery-like possibility of hitting a block makes it an exciting option.
Self-Sovereignty – Running your own node and mining solo ensures full control over your mining process and rewards.

Where to Buy a Bitaxe?

DTV Electronics Official Amazon Store
The best place to purchase a Bitaxe is DTV Electronics, where we manufacture and distribute our miners with quality and efficiency in mind. Here’s why buying from us is the best choice:

Direct from the Manufacturer – We build our Bitaxe devices ourselves, using custom cases and optimized configurations to ensure maximum performance and reliability.
Flexible Payment Options – Pay with Bitcoin on our official store or use fiat through our Amazon storefronts worldwide.
Fast, Hassle-Free Shipping – Thanks to our collaboration with Amazon, we ship globally while avoiding customs delays. With fulfillment through Amazon’s warehouses, you get quick delivery with no extra fees.
1-Year Warranty & Easy Returns – If your Bitaxe arrives dead on arrival (DOA) or develops a fault within the warranty period, simply ship it back—we’ll handle the rest.
Ordering from DTV Electronics ensures you get an authentic, high-quality Bitaxe with reliable support. Ready to start mining? Visit our store today!
